marketState: neutral, bearish technical tilt short-term but building bullish skew longer-term
summary: Front price ($2.867) sits right on the model's fair value ($2.923 now, drifting up to $2.997 by +21D) across every horizon, so there is no large mispricing to trade off the fair-value band alone. Price is running well below its typical seasonal path (-15.8% YTD vs -6.9% median), consistent with a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs the 5-year average and a bearish +22 Bcf storage-build surprise this week. Medium-term weather is the standout bullish signal: demand 8-16 days out is +22.3% vs the 5-year norm and sitting near the top of its historical range (rangePos 0.90), while 1-7D demand is only marginally above normal (+1.7%). Technicals are uniformly bearish from 1h through 1w (bearish trend, 50<200 EMA cross, negative MACD on 1h/1d/1w), but managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded, squeeze-prone position that argues against chasing fresh shorts. Seasonality reliability is explicitly labeled low (59pp 10-90 spread), so it is treated as a minor input rather than a primary driver in either direction.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, scale in over 2 tranches
longTerm.rationale: Managed-money short positioning at the 8th percentile of 5 years is historically a contrarian bullish setup, and it lines up with 8-16D demand running +22.3% vs 5y near the top of its range plus a fair-value curve that itself rises from $2.923 to $2.997 into the 21-day window, all pointing to fundamentals firming into the heart of cooling season.
longTerm.invalidation: A close below the $2.55-2.60 area, a fresh record-high storage surplus vs 5y, or 8-16D demand rolling back toward normal would invalidate the long-side skew.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk given squeeze risk
shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is only marginally above normal (no near-term bullish catalyst) while trend is bearish and 50<200 EMA-crossed on every timeframe from 1h to 1w with negative MACD on 1h/1d/1w, and the front/next futures spread is flat-to-slightly-backwardated (-$0.032), so near-term price action favors the prevailing downtrend.
shortTerm.invalidation: A reclaim and hold above $2.98-3.00, or an early verification of the hot 8-16D outlook pulling forward into realized 1-7D demand, would invalidate the short and risk a squeeze given the crowded short COT positioning.
